briefdescription.para: The main entry point for the EmacLite driver PHY loop back example in interrupt mode.

detaileddescription.para: This function will transmit/receive the frame using MAC interface loop back (in the PHY device) and verify the data in the received frame.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the EmacLite device

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS to indicate success, otherwise XST_FAILURE.

briefdescription.para: This function sends a frame of given size.

detaileddescription.para: This function assumes interrupt mode and sends the frame.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XEmacLite instance to be worked on.
is the size of the frame to create. The size only reflects the payload size, it does not include the Ethernet header size (14 bytes) nor the Ethernet CRC size (4 bytes).
if the address of the remote hardware the frame is to be sent to.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful, a driver-specific return code if not.

briefdescription.para: This function receives a frame of given size.

detaileddescription.para: This function assumes interrupt mode, receives the frame and verifies its contents.

parameterdescription.para: is the size of the frame to receive. The size only reflects the payload size, it does not include the Ethernet header size (14 bytes) nor the Ethernet CRC size (4 bytes).

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful, a driver-specific return code if not.

briefdescription.para: This function handles the receive callback from the EmacLite driver.

parameterdescription.para: is the call back reference provided to the Handler.

briefdescription.para: This function handles the transmit callback from the EmacLite driver.

parameterdescription.para: is the call back reference provided to the Handler.

briefdescription.para: This function is the main function of the EmacLite PHY loop back example in interrupt mode.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS to indicate success, otherwise XST_FAILURE.

briefdescription.para: This file contains a example for using EmacLite hardware and driver.

detaileddescription.para: This file contains an interrupt example outlining the use of interrupts and callbacks in the transmission/reception of Ethernet frames using MAC loop back in the PHY device with an incrementing payload from 1 byte to 1500 bytes (excluding Ethernet Header and FCS).
This example assumes that there is an interrupt controller in the hardware system and the EmacLite device is connected to the interrupt controller.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
2.00a ktn 02/25/09 First release 2.01a ktn 07/20/09 Updated the example to wait for either Transmit complete or Rx packet reception. 3.00a ktn 10/22/09 Updated the example to use the HAL APIs/macros. Updated example to use the macros that have been changed in the driver to remove _m from the name of the macro. 3.01a ktn 07/08/10 Updated example to support Little Endian MicroBlaze. 4.3 ms 01/23/17 Added xil_printf statement in main function to ensure that "Successfully ran" and "Failed" strings are available in all examples. This is a fix for CR-965028.
